rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting — naming a thing with a platform path/term is itself an assertion.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The handoff prompt's hard-coded skill path agents-that-build-agents-handoff.md:14 ('It's at ~/.claude/skills/session-shaper/SKILL.md') is NOT a fabrication: it matches the canonical upstream contract. spot-gaps-build-the-loop-5.md:15 declares produces.location = ~/.claude/skills/session-shaper/SKILL.md, and the training-architecture tables pin the same literal — spot-gaps-build-the-loop.md:98 (Session-shaper / second skill, ~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md) and trainer-modules.md:215 ('one SKILL.md at ~/.claude/skills/session-shaper/SKILL.md'). The requires/produces back-reference resolves: handoff.md:7-9 requires second-authored-skill from prompt:spot-gaps-build-the-loop-5, which produces id second-authored-skill. The 'session-shaper' name is curriculum-fixed by design (M6 authors it under that name), so the path the agent is told to Read is the path the upstream prompt writes. NOTE: the name-collision-on-replay risk (returning/multi-SUT student already holds a session-shaper skill -> Claude suffix-renames -> hard-coded literal misses) is a real but already-LOGGED-AND-ACCEPTED watch for first cohort: pre-cohort-todos.md:21 and :37. Within the first-cohort scope this passes; the watch is the standing mitigation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known-correct Claude Code facts — verify slash-command names against canonical docs.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lectures/agents-that-build-agents.md:45 'Claude Code shipped `/goal`. The runtime version of the same move. A condition, a check each turn.' is verified, not memory-asserted. /goal is live-confirmed against Claude Code 2.1.142: commands page lists `/goal [condition|clear]`; `claude -p '/goal test condition'` executes; no-arg returns usage hint. Corroborated in source-sweep-2026-05-25/group-E.md:150 and group-B.md:194 (the latter records the live-verification was done in the 2026-05-15 pass, treated as already-verified). The body framing — a condition, a check each turn — is observable-behavior-only and does NOT overclaim a worker/judge model split (that was explicitly left un-live-tested in the prior pass and stays out of the body). This Ralph section is unchanged by the close swap; re-confirmed consistent. The body is also consistent with reference_claude_code_capabilities.md (which lists /loop as the in-session loop primitive; /goal is the condition-check primitive, a distinct command — no contradiction).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork; no claude.ai-only leaning.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body and prompt name Claude Code only (lectures:21 'Claude proposes, you steer'; :39 bash one-liner pipes to claude-code; handoff.md frontmatter dest: Claude Code, runtime: any). AE101 is CLI+Desktop-canonical (curriculum/CLAUDE.md Platform claims) — naming the product is in-bounds. No claude.ai-only feature relied on.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Runtime sandbox — ~/.claude writes/reads suspect on sandboxed runtimes (Cowork); valid on CLI/Desktop.
rules_evaluated.evidence: handoff.md is dest: Claude Code (CLI+Desktop), runtime: any — not Cowork. The ~/.claude/skills/ read (handoff.md:14) and skill-authoring are within CLI/Desktop filesystem scope; skills auto-discover from ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md per reference_claude_code_capabilities.md. produces.location = scrollback (standalone handoff prompt), not a sandbox-illegal write. No Cowork runtime-fork required for AE101 (AE101 has no Cowork per curriculum/CLAUDE.md).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cold-runnable prompt — capability claimed (read skill from disk, study cross-repo work, author skills) must be real, no overclaim.
rules_evaluated.evidence: The capabilities the handoff prompt asks Claude Code to perform are all within real CLI/Desktop scope and not overclaimed. (1) Read a skill from a disk path (handoff.md:14) — ordinary Read tool. (2) 'study my work across my repos for the shapes I repeat' (handoff.md:16) — multi-directory filesystem read the agent does when pointed at those dirs; NOT a claim of automatic transcript-mining or hidden cross-session memory (no session-transcript-reading capability is asserted in body or prompt). (3) 'author a skill for each shape' — skill authoring is a real Write into ~/.claude/skills/. (4) Cold-runnable: handoff.md:18 'It can't lean on this session or this repo. Put it in a code block so I can save it and run it cold' — the produced artifact is standalone and the skill is referenced by explicit disk path (cold-safe, no scrollback dependence), matching the maintainer note at lectures:83. No fabricated Claude Code feature.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-verification maintainer block + freshness stamp on practitioner citations; capability claim is a source whose 'open' is a live test (11a).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Huntley/Ralph citation carries the required stamp (lectures:76): `[checked:2026-05-25 result:OK due:2026-11-25]` <https://ghuntley.com/ralph/> — [practitioner direct], named fallback, grammar matches 11a. The Ralph section is outside the close-swap surface and its prior technical PASS recorded the /goal live-verification; logging the stamp into the maintainer block is a tidy-up, not a blocking citation defect. Routed to todos.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Freshness — 6 months; older cited as dated historical context only.

Maintainer block flags it at lectures:73 ('outside 6-month freshness, cite as origin not current evidence') and lectures:76 ('origin/historical only, body flags it correctly'). rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Vision x Detail split — detail-layer claims (shipped practices, named techniques) need KB backing.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Detail-layer claims are backed. (1) Huntley origin (bash loop verbatim, Simpsons name) — stamped + KB-corroborated (continuous-research has the Huntley/Ralph thread; source-sweep group-B.md:194 confirms byline + loop + name). (2) '/goal shipped' — live-verified 2.1.142 (rule 6). (3) 'Ralph re-feed entered the practitioner vocabulary as one of the named verifier shapes' (lectures:43) — the verifier-shape framing is the lecture's own vision-layer synthesis (Antti's arc, attributed to the practitioner pattern Module 5/6 already named), built on the KB's practitioner-convergence thread (continuous-research/synthesis/practitioner-convergent-stance.md, workflow-composition findings carry Huntley); reads as governing-rule framing of a real practitioner pattern, not an unbacked statistic or fabricated named technique. Within the Vision x Detail split this is acceptable.
